deck.title: Knowledge Cat PPT Operating System
deck.audience: AI creators and operators who need repeatable presentation workflows
deck.outcome: Adopt a story-first slide workflow instead of asking for pretty slides first
deck.main_promise: A deck becomes reliable when story, engine, design, and QA are treated as one system
deck.narrative_spine: Problem -> failed old approach -> new mechanism -> proof -> next step
assumptions: The user needs an editable professional deck
The deck is a short internal explanation, not a public keynote

slides.action_title: Most AI decks fail because they start with style instead of audience shift
slides.key_message: The root problem is not decoration, but unclear outcome and story.
evidence: Observed failure pattern from presentation skill benchmarks
slides.visual: Split comparison: prompt-first slide pile versus brief-first deck system
slides.speaker_note: Open with the common failure mode and name the alternative.

slides.action_title: A production deck needs four decisions before slide generation starts
slides.key_message: Audience, story, output lane, and QA level determine every downstream choice.
slides.speaker_note: Show that this is a controllable process, not taste magic.

slides.action_title: Native PPTX, HTML, and image-first decks solve different jobs
slides.key_message: Choosing the wrong output lane creates avoidable downstream failure.
slides.speaker_note: Make the tradeoffs explicit so users can choose intentionally.

slides.action_title: Action titles turn a slide deck into a readable argument
slides.key_message: The ghost deck test exposes weak story structure before design work begins.
evidence: Action title and ghost deck standards
slides.speaker_note: Contrast topic labels with complete-sentence claims.

slides.action_title: Rendered QA is what turns a good plan into a shippable deck
slides.key_message: The final quality gate must inspect real output, not just the plan or code.
slides.visual: Checklist with content, visual, story, and format QA gates
slides.speaker_note: Close by making QA part of the workflow, not an afterthought.
